problem_addressed: Scientific discovery is a lengthy and costly process that limits the number of research ideas researchers can explore.
core_claim: Agent Laboratory is an autonomous LLM-based framework that can complete the entire research process from idea to final results, significantly reducing research expenses and improving quality.
key_contributions: Introduction of Agent Laboratory framework capable of completing entire research process through literature review, experimentation, and report writing
Demonstration that Agent Laboratory achieves state-of-the-art performance in machine learning code generation
Evidence that human feedback at each stage significantly improves research quality
Finding that Agent Laboratory reduces research expenses by 84% compared to previous autonomous research methods
novelty: Creating a comprehensive autonomous framework that handles the entire research pipeline using specialized LLM-driven agents, rather than focusing on just one aspect of research.
method_summary: Agent Laboratory accepts a human-provided research idea and progresses through three stages—literature review, experimentation, and report writing—to produce comprehensive research outputs including a code repository and research report, while enabling user feedback at each stage.
method_components: User feedback mechanism at each stage
key_innovation: The pipeline of specialized LLM-driven agents that work together to complete the entire research process autonomously while allowing for human feedback and guidance at each stage.
assumptions: LLMs can effectively perform literature review, experimentation, and report writing tasks
Human feedback at each stage can meaningfully improve research outcomes
The generated code will achieve state-of-the-art performance
theoretical_grounding: None provided - relies on empirical evaluation through experiments and human assessments.
title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
